fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maryland Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity maryland, how to transfer occupational therapist license to maryland, occupational therapist compact maryland, maryland occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Maryland Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.mailing_address:
Maryland Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
4201 Patterson Avenue
Baltimore, MD 21215-2299

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Maryland.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create OPLS Account and Apply
steps.description: Register for an account on the Maryland Online Professional Licensing System (OPLS) portal and complete the online licensure application, submitting the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official transcripts, verifying completion of an accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Maryland Board of Occupational Therapy Practice.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Ensure your passing scores for the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination are sent directly from NBCOT to the Maryland Board of Occupational Therapy Practice.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Records Check
steps.description: Undergo a criminal history records check by submitting fingerprints as directed by the Board. Specific instructions for fingerprinting will be provided during the OPLS application process.

steps.title: Provide Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit any other supporting documentation requested by the Board, which may include verification of licensure from other states, professional liability insurance, or other forms, either through OPLS or directly to the Board.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once all required documentation and information are received and verified, the Board will review your application. Upon approval, your Maryland Occupational Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maryland OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Maryland have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Maryland has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Maryland?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Maryland
hero.dek: Users can apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Maryland by registering and submitting their application through the Maryland Online Professional Licensing System (OPLS).

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To begin your application, visit the Maryland Online Professional Licensing System (OPLS) portal. If you are a new user, click 'Register for an Account' and follow the prompts to create your profile. Once registered, log in and select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Initial Application' or 'Endorsement Application') to start.
